rulings.Case Title: WALTER MORRIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Good Faith Settlement (Ccp Sec. 877.6(A)(1)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Will Milam Enterprises, Inc.¿s motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA ANN MELLO, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ROBARE VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Defendant Century Lumber Centers Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Summons And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Specially appearing defendant Century Lumber Center¿s motion to quash service of summons is taken off calendar as moot due to the court¿s June 15, 2012 orders staying this case pursuant to defendants¿ motions to stay on the grounds of forum non conveniens. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LAVON HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Groeniger & Co.¿s motion to strike: Defendant¿s motion to strike each cause of action for plaintiffs¿ failure to file a declaration in conformity with C.C.P. section 377.32 is granted with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to submit papers to meet the requirements of C.C.P. section 377.32. Defendant¿s motion to strike the complaint in its entirety based on the doctrine of res judicata is off calendar as moot given the court¿s ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. Defendant¿s motion to strike portions of the complaint regarding the claim for punitive damages and agency allegations is off calendar as moot given the ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LAVON HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al

rulings.Case Title: LAVON HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Groeniger & Co.¿s demurrer: Defendant¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ entire complaint based on the doctrine of res judicata is overruled. All the requirements to apply that doctrine are not met here, namely that there be a final judgment in the prior action. Defendant¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ entire complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EMIL DOLNER et al VS. PNEUMO ABEX LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant McCord Corporation¿s motion to change venue is denied without prejudice. Defendant failed to demonstrate that San Francisco is not the proper court and failed to demonstrate that the convenience of the witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by a change of venue as required by C.C.P. section 397(a) and (c). Plaintiffs¿ objection to defendant¿s notice of motion is overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
